description: When you know yourself, you know yourself twice over.

hints.text: When your goal is an [implication|implication] $A \to B$, the standard approach is to assume the left-hand side and then prove the right-hand side. Try starting there.
Assume $A$ to bring it into your [context|context]. To prove $A \land A$, you'll need two copies of the same truth — one for each side of the $\land$.
The `and-intro` tactic accepts two step numbers and produces their [conjunction|conjunction]. Nothing says those two steps can't be the **same** step.
You've proven $A \land A$ while assuming $A$. Now use `->-intro` to [discharge|discharge] that [assumption|assumption] and wrap everything into the [implication|implication] you need.
